headline: Late Night Legacy: Colbert's Star-Studded 'Late Show' Farewell Assembles Comedy Royalty
lede: Stephen Colbert prepares to close the curtain on his landmark late-night run with a parade of comedy titans set to make cameo appearances during his final week.
body:
CBS's long-running late-night institution is set to receive a blockbuster sendoff as Stephen Colbert concludes his transformative nine-year tenure. David Letterman, Colbert's legendary predecessor, headlines a remarkable roster of comedic talent converging to celebrate the host's final broadcast on May 21.

The finale promises a retrospective journey through Colbert's most memorable moments, featuring surprise appearances from current and former late-night luminaries. Industry insiders suggest this farewell will be less a conclusion and more a celebration of Colbert's significant impact on political satire and television comedy.

Colbert's transition represents more than a personal milestone — it signals a broader shift in late-night television's evolving landscape. His razor-sharp political commentary and intellectual humor redefined the genre, moving beyond traditional monologue structures into more incisive cultural critique.

Network executives remain tight-lipped about specific details, but early indications suggest a star-studded tribute that will honor Colbert's remarkable run. The comedian's ability to blend humor, political insight, and genuine emotional connection has made him a standout in a crowded late-night ecosystem.
key_facts: David Letterman will make a guest appearance
Colbert hosted 'Late Show' for nine years
Multiple late-night hosts expected to participate
show_title: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert
